channel.title: Mohit Mehta
channel.description: Less theory, more decision clarity around what matters. Management innovation basics meeting start-up reality.

item.title: The fallacy of "If we build it, they will come"
item.description: 10 or so years back, I heard this phrase the first time in a TWIST podcast episode from Jason Calacanis and got intrigued. He did mention it in a cautionary sense and I got intrigued.

item.title: Organisational Harmony - When Teams Move as One
item.description: It is the combined actions of a collective which determines where it goes.

item.title: The "Land and Expand" Strategy
item.description: Enterprise Sales are not easy. They are complex on most of the vectors of opportunity, longer sales cycles and of course a multiplicity of stakeholders. This is where the ”Land and Expand” comes in. A wedge in the…

item.title: Generative AI - Debunking Myths for Leaders
item.description: Generative AI is quickly emerging as a transformative technology, capturing the imagination of businesses and individuals alike. As executives, it's crucial to separate fact from fiction and understand the true…

item.title: Generative AI - The New Probabilistic Shift
item.description: We are standing at the edge of a new world where AI is actively transforming business landscapes. This transformation isn't just theoretical; it's a tangible revolution in how we approach customer engagement, risk…

item.title: Generating Advantage through Constraints
item.description: In the ever evolving landscape of business, constraints are often viewed as unwelcome barriers to growth and creativity. Yet, what if these very limitations are the hidden keys to unlocking unprecedented innovation and…

item.title: Stability and Change - The Yin and Yang of Progress
item.description: In the ever evolving landscape of business, technology, and society, the concepts of stability and change often seem to be at odds with each other. However, a closer look reveals that they are not just complementary but…

item.title: First ABC then PQR - Building on solid foundations
item.description: In today's fast paced business landscape, the temptation to leap ahead—to innovate, disrupt, and scale—often overshadows the significance of robust foundations for lasting progress. This post aims to shed light on an…

item.title: Strategic Wandering - Balancing Exploration and Experience
item.description: Exploration is like a double edged sword. On one hand, it can open up new opportunities, inspire innovation, and offer fresh perspectives. On the other hand, without focused objectives, it can become an open ended…

item.title: The AI Decision - Open-source or Proprietary
item.description: In the fast paced world of business, leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI) is no longer optional—it's a necessity. But one question often stumps decision makers: Should you go for a closed, proprietary AI system or an…

item.title: Startups - Cultivating Trust through Positioning and Transparency
item.description: The legendary Peter Drucker once remarked, “The purpose of a business is to create a customer.” For startups, this raises the question: who exactly is this customer?”

item.title: The Game of Strategy - Timing and Market Choice
item.description: Growing a business requires grit and grind, both of which are non negotiable. However, timing and luck also play a significant role in it’s success. This post explores these factors, with particular emphasis on the…

item.title: From Me to We - Navigating the Shift in Creative Intentions
item.description: The act of creation, of bringing something new into the world, is a thrilling experience. Whether it's a work of art, a fresh piece of code, a solution oriented product, or the construction of a new house, the joy of…

item.title: When does Activity translate into Productivity?
item.description: Life is very busy these days. Activity has become a synonym for progress. If we take a pause, it feels we are not progressing.

item.title: The value of last 5%
item.description: A good product is more than a collection of features.

item.title: Why positioning matters for startups?
item.description: Every startup has a positioning. This positioning makes their identity. It is foundational literally and helps the startup generate a stance.

item.title: How do the winds blow in your organisation?
item.description: Culture is NOT a set of values statement chosen by an organisation, or something seen in the group/offsite activities they may engage in once in a while, BUT it is the collective set of behaviours practiced within the…

item.title: What does Great mean in a Great product?
item.description: Its ability to solve a specific and important problem in a simple way... Yes, exactly.

item.title: Decentralisation and Purpose
item.description: A few years back I had read a note on the future of work by Thomas Malone, a professor at MIT.

item.title: Myths around the AI bandwagon
item.description: Every start up today wants to be an AI start up. That's great.

item.title: Idea selection over Idea generation
item.description: Yes experiments are now cheaper to do but does not mean every question is worth investigation. Is it?

item.title: Good chaos... Bad chaos
item.description: Startups and chaos go hand in hand, as they work in a dynamic environments, continuously adapt and are on a journey.

item.title: Some challenges around Deep Learning networks
item.description: Machine learning is paving the way for Artificial Intelligence. With ever increasing amounts of data around us and the availability of cheaper and faster compute, its specific applications are only limited by our…

item.title: Focus and Startups
item.description: Focus is central to every startup... but probably not for the reasons you think.

item.title: Clarity as a Competitive Advantage
item.description: When the ask is clear, progress is faster. Why is that so?

item.title: Are you listening
item.description: Effective listening means tuning in to all verbal and non verbal cues. It means being empathetic to converge on what is really being said.

item.title: Work and Play
item.description: Why does work feel taxing and stressful, while play rejuvenates? Does it have to be so, what is missing and what can we do?

item.title: Fruits of Passion
item.description: Beginning with a quote from the legendary Peter Drucker:
